title: Logs command honors the --lines limit
description: Black-box smoke for `orca logs --lines <n>` with a preseeded agent log.
input: Seed an agent log with three lines, run `orca logs --lines 1`, and verify the output only includes the newest log entry.
command:
bash -lc 'REPO_ROOT=${PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}; cd "$REPO_ROOT"; bash agent-eval/scripts/prepare-fast-gate.sh >/dev/null; TMP_ROOT=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP_ROOT" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP_ROOT/home/.orca/logs"; cat > "$TMP_ROOT/home/.orca/logs/agent.log" <<EOF
[INFO] one
[INFO] two
[INFO] three
EOF
OUTPUT=$(env -i PATH="$PATH" HOME="$TMP_ROOT/home" ORCA_HOME="$TMP_ROOT/home/.orca" node dist/bin/orca.js logs --lines 1); printf "%s\n" "$OUTPUT"; ! printf "%s" "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "\[INFO\] one"; ! printf "%s" "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "\[INFO\] two"; printf "logs-lines-ok\n"'
